chapters.summary: The latest episode of the Indie Hackers podcast features an episode on cryptocurrencies. Courtland is convinced that nfts could be the future of the web. But nothing contained in this episode is financial advice. Do your own research.
chapters.gist: Indie Hackers: Cryptocurrency Episode 1
chapters.headline: Courtland is doing an episode on cryptocurrencies, specifically nfts

chapters.summary: Mubasar Iqbal: I've been dabbling with nfts for a while. Heaton: I have over a thousand, if I count them across all the chains. One I'm very focused on, which is a funded business.
chapters.gist: Meet The NFT Team
chapters.headline: Heat and Shah and Mubasar Iqbal discuss the NFT space

chapters.summary: Nfts are very lucrative. If I were to tell anyone how to make money like level one, buy the coins, just buy like a week. You can make money, but you can also lose a lot of money.
chapters.gist: How to Make Money Trading Nfts
chapters.headline: You can make money by buying and selling nfts like this

chapters.summary: Web three is the evolution of how we host and we do infrastructure online. Now it's just hosted in the cloud. So that fundamentally is what has me interested and why I think it's the future.
chapters.gist: Top Executives: The Cloud Is the Future
chapters.headline: Was there ever a time where you were skeptics about nfts

chapters.summary: With the blockchain, what a decentralized ledger is, is it's a decentralized database instead of existing on one computer somewhere on the Internet. Why aren't you skeptical about nfts? Why are you dabbling in this space?
chapters.gist: Bitcoin and the Future of Nfts
chapters.headline: Gary Dy: Blockchain is a decentralized ledger that exists across thousands of computers

chapters.summary: NFT is a non fungible token. It's essentially digital property. Non fungible means each individual in one is its own distinct thing. The killer use case for this right now is people creating digital items in the form of art and collectibles.
chapters.gist: Non-Fully-Flexible Tokens
chapters.headline: Bitcoin and ethereum are fungible tokens that exist on a blockchain

chapters.summary: The technology of NFTs enables us to open up the world a little bit. I can now use anybody's NFT inside of my own platform because it's available for public on the ledger with an open API. It has huge implications to how e commerce works, how commerce works not just in this online world.
chapters.gist: Will NFTs Make Indie Games More Accessible?
chapters.headline: NFTs could revolutionize how e commerce works in the real world

chapters.summary: With nfTs, it's all digital. Anyone can look on the blockchain and see that. it was actually Twitter who issued me that NFT. NFTs really do allow ownership. The reason I'm into this has nothing to do with anything except sense of belonging.
chapters.gist: NFTs: Ownership, Access and Sense of Belong
chapters.headline: NFTs really do allow ownership, access, and sense of belonging

chapters.summary: It's not just about the content. It's about the community that you've built on that platform. I don't necessarily see a world in which people abandon Twitter or current social networks en masse because they don't own their stuff. I see a path where people, bottom up, start using new apps and new websites.
chapters.gist: Will We Abandon Twitter and Facebook?
chapters.headline: I don't necessarily see a world in which people abandon Twitter or current social networks

chapters.summary: Internet visionaries see a future of telecommuting workers and interactive libraries. Do computer pundits lack all common sense? Everything he predicted was wrong. Even if you're very savvy, these futuristic predictions might look inaccurate at the time.
chapters.gist: The Future of the Internet Was Predictions in 1995
chapters.headline: A Newsweek article from 1995 argued against predictions of what the Internet would do

chapters.summary: The ownership of digital assets in the video game industry is an absolute certainty in my mind. People are already buying sports collectibles as nfts already. How do you know that people will keep collecting things? Ultimately depends on what they're buying.
chapters.gist: Future of Digital Property: Possessing Digital Assets
chapters.headline: What do you see digital property enabling in the future of nfts

chapters.summary: Mark Cuban is a big crypto junkie. NFT projects are in the Facebook apps phase, right? It might all disappear. But tickets and access hit those three vectors. Some of the use cases are sort of unimaginable.
chapters.gist: Can You Predict the Future of NFT?
chapters.headline: Keaton: I don't like to predict the future from a prediction standpoint

chapters.summary: The items you put on the blockchain as NFTs are portable because the blockchain is publicly accessible. This opens up the creator economy to everybody on the planet. And plus, that stuff will outlive your company.
chapters.gist: The Blockchain and the Creator Economy
chapters.headline: Putting digital items on the blockchain unlocks a whole host of possibilities for creators

chapters.summary: Chris Dixon: The marketing they do is just not traditional marketing. It's community marketing. The only time I saw such a tight feedback loop, whether it's NFTs or whatever, was clubhouse. The secondary system here on the blockchain is going to flip a lot more stuff upside down.
chapters.gist: Chris Dixon On Community
chapters.headline: Chris Dixon says cryptocurrency marketing is not traditional marketing

chapters.summary: Lets talk about how you can actually invest in this as a founder today or as anybody. Number one, buy tokens. Number two, get into DeFi. Number three, explore this crazy world of nfTs. Is there any room as like a founder?
chapters.gist: How to Invest in Crypto as a SaaS Founder
chapters.headline: Lets talk about how you can actually invest in this as a founder today

chapters.summary: I would love to see a world where almost everything is represented as an NFT. That tells me something about the identity of the people who come to my website. It takes time to, because those are old, established industries and technologies that are just going to wake up to what the new reality is.
chapters.gist: What They Want to See on Founder Path
chapters.headline: I'll tell you what I want to see with founder Path. If a founder goes to founder Path and they raise

chapters.summary: Heaton: What if someone could access a document with their kind of metamask and their crypto credentials instead of their email. If I were a SaaS business, there is this NFT concept where it's like, access and all the stuff. But I don't see how you would want to jam that into your business.
chapters.gist: Google's File Security: Securing Your Business
chapters.headline: Heaton is working on security for file storage on the blockchain

chapters.summary: Simple idea with the community is people can tip each other with crypto somehow. Is does someone already have a wallet or not? Do they have they already jumped through those hoops? If they haven't, is it your job to train them?
chapters.gist: Should a Crypto Community be Built?
chapters.headline: Simple idea with community is people can tip each other with crypto somehow

chapters.summary: Cortlandt: Is there a particular website they should trust? Where do you go if you want to put in this hundred hours of learning? Find a buddy. If you're really doing it for savings long term, don't put a lot of money in there.
chapters.gist: Buying Crypto With a Trustful Friend
chapters.headline: Cortlandt: You've said buy crypto, but what next

chapters.summary: All right, Heaton mobs, thanks so much for coming on. Can you let listeners know where they can go to find out more about what you're up to with your SaaS businesses. You can find me on Twitter and all the usual social media places.
chapters.gist: In the Elevator With Ashirik Bal
chapters.headline: Heaton and Hnshah are both working on SaaS businesses

episode_title: #235 – An NFT Conversation for Indie Hackers with Hiten Shah and Mubashar Iqbal
